titleStmt.title: Naameh – AD (F.67) – Maslakh Before (3)
WIBARAB Corpus
titleStmt.funder: Funded by the European Research Council under the Grant Agreement 101020127

publicationStmt.distributor: Austrian Center for Digital Humanities and Cultural Heritage

recording.p: Recorded during Lebanon Fieldwork Campaign Ana Iriarte Diez 2023

prefixDef.p: Private URIs using the teiCorpusHeader prefix are pointers to any element in the WIBARAB teiCorpus document.
Private URIs using the share prefix are pointers to audio file residing on the WIBARAB network share.
Private URIs using the geo prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a place element in VICAV Geodata list.
Private URIs using the dmp prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB DMP document..
Private URIs using the zotid prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a biblStruct element in the TEI export of the VICAV Zotero Group library.
Private URIs using the sources prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB list of sources.

spanGrp.span: Well. Which year exactly did you go to Tel al-Zaatar? in...
We uh...
Do you remeber?
Yes. In 1967.
1967. Did you go straight to the hotel or did you go to the... Saint Simon afterwards?
No no, we didn't go to the ...
yes.
Us, when Tel al-Zaatar fell, they took us to Aayoun El Siman.
Ah.
yes. Some families (like ours) went to Aayoun El Siman, and we came back from Aayoun El Siman ...
A man came from Shmustar, the mayor of Shmustar, he took a lot of people (with him), and among them, us.

And then we came to La Siesta (Hotel) straightaway. Those who came to the hotel.. Arab al-Maslakh before us... The people from the Maslakh area (came) to the hotel before us. Some went to the chalets and some to the hotel..
We came to La Siesta (Hotel).
to the La Siesta (Hotel).
Yes. We stayed for eighteen years. La Siesta.
But they said this year there were in La Siesta Arab al-Maslakh and Kredin in the hotel? Or was it the other way around? Or were they all together?
No no no, the... Those who were in the hotel were all Arab al-Maslakh. there was no stranger.
In the hotel (were) Arab al-Maslakh, yes.
All of it, there was no stranger.
ah in La Siesta everyone was Kredin. and Aransa and Lahib, and Khlayfat, yes.
but primarily there were Arab Kredin there.
in La Siesta yes, most of them were Kredin.
And... in the hotel there was no one. All of them were Arab al-Maslakh. There was no stranger amongst them.
But eighteen years is a lot.
Eighteen years we stayed in La Siesta.
How did it life at the hotel feel like?
in La Siesta? Good.
yes. Was it like in Maslakh?
No. God forbid (I take refuge in God)! I do not compare.
Not at all
They are not comparable, God forbid (I take refuge in God)! it was good. the... I mean in La Siesta people on the beach, people were inside (lit. in the room).
inside. people started to build their balconies. Those with families.
I got you.
No, there was no joy in La Siesta.

I mean, imagine someone leaving his house and his region and goes and migrates out of it, and to a neighborhood other than his, how will he find this (new neighborhood)? How can it be? His whole life changes.
right.
Also it was all so... All the bombing and the incidents. So many young lives were lost... Life is very hard.
Right. Yes, it is true.
